back_translation: Samson went down to Timnah and saw a woman in Timnah, of the daughters of the Philistines.
He came up and told his father and his mother and said, 'I have seen a woman in Timnah, of the daughters of the Philistines. Now take her for me as a wife.'

His father and his mother said to him, 'Is there not a woman among the daughters of your brothers and among all our people, that you go to take a wife from the uncircumcised Philistines?' Samson said to his father, 'Take her for me, for she is pleasing in my eyes.'
His father and his mother did not know that this was from the LORD, for He was seeking an occasion against the Philistines. At that time the Philistines were ruling over Israel.
Samson went down, with his father and his mother, to Timnah, and they came to the vineyards of Timnah. Behold, a young lion came roaring to meet him.
The Spirit of the LORD rushed upon him, and he tore the lion apart as one tears apart a young goat, with nothing in his hand. He did not tell his father or his mother what he had done.
Samson went down and spoke to the woman, and she was pleasing in the eyes of Samson.
After some time he returned to take her, and he turned aside to see the carcass of the lion. Behold, there was a swarm of bees in the body of the lion, and honey.

He scraped some into his hands and went on eating. He came to his father and his mother and gave to them, and they ate. But he did not tell them that he had scraped the honey from the carcass of the lion.
His father went down to the woman, and Samson made a feast there, for so the young men used to do.
When they saw him, they took thirty companions to be with him.

Samson said to them, 'Let me now pose a riddle to you. If you can indeed tell me its answer within the seven days of the feast and find it out, I will give you thirty linen garments and thirty changes of clothing.'
'But if you cannot tell me, then you shall give me thirty linen garments and thirty changes of clothing.' They said to him, 'Pose your riddle, that we may hear it.'
He said to them, 'Out of the eater came something to eat, and out of the strong came something sweet.' For three days they could not tell the riddle.
On the fourth day they said to Samson's wife, 'Entice your husband so that he tells us the riddle, lest we burn you and your father's house with fire. Did you invite us here to rob us?'

Samson's wife wept over him and said, 'You only hate me and do not love me. You have posed a riddle to the sons of my people, but you have not told me.' He said to her, 'Behold, I have not told my father or my mother — and should I tell you?'
She wept over him for the seven days during which their feast lasted. On the seventh day he told her, because she had pressed him. She told the riddle to the sons of her people.

The men of the city said to him on the seventh day before the sun went down, 'What is sweeter than honey, and what is stronger than a lion?' He said to them, 'If you had not plowed with my heifer, you would not have found out my riddle.'

The Spirit of the LORD rushed upon him, and he went down to Ashkelon and struck down thirty of their men, took their garments, and gave the changes of clothing to those who told the riddle. His anger burned, and he went up to his father's house.
Samson's wife was given to his companion who had been his friend.
